fresh_facts.claim: U.S. and Israeli forces conducted intensive airstrikes on Iranian missile production sites, IRGC infrastructure, command centers, and ports across Tehran and multiple provinces during March 27-29, 2026.
The USS Tripoli was deployed with approximately 3,500 Marines for amphibious and air assault operations in the Middle East.
U.S. Central Command reports over 11,000 targets struck since operations began, including IRGC bases, missile systems, and more than 150 Iranian vessels damaged or destroyed.
Pentagon officials are preparing contingency plans for limited ground operations in Iran involving Special Operations forces and infantry raids, short of full invasion.
Israeli forces conducted extensive strikes over March 27-29, targeting strategic, military, and nuclear sites across Iran with intensified bombardment in Tehran affecting multiple districts.
Israeli strikes targeted IRGC command centers, intelligence facilities, ammunition depots, weapons storage, and communications nodes to dismantle command-and-control architecture.
Iranian missile capabilities have been degraded following strikes on production sites, mobile and fixed command centers, and defense industry zones.
Iran conducted drone strikes on Kurdish opposition groups in Iraq's Kurdish region and targeted Gulf economic facilities including Aluminium Bahrain and a UAE aluminum site.
An unverified Iranian strike reportedly destroyed a U.S. airborne early warning aircraft at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia.
President Trump suspended strikes on Iranian power plants for 10 days until April 6, 2026 at 8 p.m. Eastern time, following what he described as a request from the Iranian government.

Trump has maintained threats to 'completely obliterate' Iranian electrical generating plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly desalination plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened and a deal is not reached.
According to Trump, the U.S. is engaged in 'serious discussions' with what he called 'a new regime in Iran' to end military operations.
Iran has consistently signaled that peace talks are not progressing and indicated it can sustain the conflict for much longer.
Iran's ruling system 'appears more fragile than at any point in its recent history,' with the clerical establishment 'struggling for survival' rather than projecting confidence.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed as secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, signaling a security-first approach to governance prioritizing control over reform.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr's appointment was made months after mass killings during January 2026 protests, confirming the regime's commitment to internal security measures rather than political change.
Maryam Rajavi presented a Ten-Point Plan on March 15, 2026, framing it as a constitutional blueprint for Iran's democratic transition.

Maryam Rajavi's Ten-Point Plan has reportedly received endorsement from over a thousand lawmakers and international figures, establishing the principle that 'sovereignty belongs exclusively to the people.'
The conflict has moved into a 'grinding middle phase' where neither battlefield momentum nor diplomacy has become decisive.
Kuwait announced the arrest of six people linked to Hezbollah accused of plotting to assassinate state leaders.
The Iran war's economic fallout is cascading well beyond the Persian Gulf and the Middle East, reshaping markets and supply chains globally.
U.S. and Israeli operations expanded to Iran-aligned Popular Mobilization Forces in Iraq's Nineveh province near Mosul.
IRGC infrastructure, including bases and logistics, faced heavy targeting with Reuters reporting ongoing airstrikes during daily life in Iran on March 29, 2026.
Tehran experienced city-wide saturation strikes on March 29, starting late evening, continuing early morning, and renewing post-sunrise, hitting military and security sites and fuel logistics.

regime_fall.current_status: Intensive U.S.-Israeli campaign has reached over 11,000 strikes with deployed amphibious forces and contingency ground operation plans, while Trump suspended strikes on power infrastructure through April 6 amid reported negotiations with 'a new regime.' Iran's ruling system faces its most fragile state in recent history with succession uncertainty, military-security dominated power shifts, cascading economic collapse, and asymmetric Iranian responses indicating neither battlefield momentum nor diplomacy has become decisive.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump suspended strikes on Iranian power plants for 10 days through April 6, 2026, while claiming 'serious discussions' with 'a new regime in Iran,' signaling a potential diplomatic inflection point amid the most intensive military campaign.
